prompt: Design a "Medical Operations and Process Governance Middle Platform API Service" that provides unified domain interface capabilities for hospital operation administrators, department reviewers, general business personnel, and auditors, with an identity domain supporting user registration/login/logout and password recovery (usernames as unique identifiers, passwords requiring at least 8 characters including both letters and numbers), users able to create/join organizations with data isolated at the organizational level, roles following a four-tier model (administrator, reviewer, general user, auditor) with permissions controlled by resource domains and operational semantics; an operations analysis domain offering key indicator dashboards and customizable reporting capabilities covering metrics such as activity, message reach, attendance anomalies, work order SLA, and multi-criteria searches with advanced filtering for appointments/patients/doctors/expenses; an export domain supporting field whitelist-based exports with desensitization policies and traceability of export task records; a process domain including two workflow types (resource application-approval-allocation and credit change approval) supporting conditional branching, joint/parallel signing, SLA time limits (default 48 hours), reminders, application material uploads and retention with approval comments, and final results written back to form a full-chain audit trail; a backend in a single offline environment using FastAPI to handle resource-level interface categorization and permission boundaries with SQLAlchemy+PostgreSQL for persistence and transaction consistency, with core data models including users, organizations, role authorizations, approval process definitions, approval instances, task assignments, attachment metadata, operational metric snapshots, and data dictionaries, plus key constraints such as unique indexes for usernames/organization codes, idempotent keys for approval instances (duplicate submissions with the same business number within 24 hours return the same processing result), status enumerations, and time field indexing; a data governance domain providing coding rules and quality validation (missing, duplicate, out-of-bounds data) with errors written back to batch details during imports, supporting data versioning/snapshots/rollbacks and lineage tracing, complemented by daily full backups, 30-day archiving, and task scheduling failure compensation (maximum 3 retries); and a security and compliance domain requiring encrypted storage of sensitive fields (ID numbers, contact information) and role-based desensitization in responses, transmission restricted to HTTPS only, all changes logged in immutable operation logs and audit trails, abnormal login attempts risk-controlled based on failure counts (5 consecutive failures within 10 minutes trigger a 30-minute lockout), file uploads validated locally for format and size (single file ≤20MB) and deduplicated via fingerprints, and attachment access requiring validation of organizational and business ownership with unauthorized reads prohibited.
